speaker: Narrator
text:
Blanche is dreading to refill the day's petrol bill; she'll have to clean the car for the smell of fish, vomit, and other excretions, and what have you, but she’ll be rid of Christina for a few days. 
	
	She reaches the address on her navigation, all the way in the armpit of a suburb of Stockholm, outside of a featureless block of apartment buildings. Some ruffians were loitering outside.
	
	She turns down the metal; it’s a song about time travelers going back in time to save Julian at Ctesiphon. It's not quite as good as the Mamluks.

speaker: Blanche
text: Oh look Christina, it’s your place.

speaker: Blanche
text: I’ll take care of most of the lab work since you’re feeling so sick.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina begins to cough hard, and grimaces from the effort.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina nods and continues to cough.

speaker: Christina
text: Thanks for the ride, I’ll see you.

speaker: Narrator
text: Blanche watches Christina limp her way up the footpath to where one of the hoodlums opens the door for her. Another one seems to walk her inside before she disappears from view.
